title: Q & A on Theory of Everything - The Vedanta Perspective (Part 1)
speaker: HG Chanchalapathi Dasa

segments.text: Om Agyanati Mirandhasya Gyananjana Shalakaya
Chakshurun Milithan Enatasmai Shri Gurave Namaha
Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale
Shimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine
Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine
Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Paschatya Deshatarine
Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda
Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shivaasadi Gauravatta Vrinda
Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare
Nice to be back with all of you.
And I'll take a few questions that have already come up
from different sources.
And I heard that there are a few more questions
which have come up today.
I'll be doing that after covering these few
that I have received earlier.
The first question is,
how to get rid of being carried away
by power of seeing through the wheel of illusion
created by modern materialistic science
by which it presents itself as the authority on the universe.
Okay, let's read this a little bit slowly.
We don't want to get carried away.
How to get rid of being carried away?
By power of seeing, power of seeing through the wheel of illusion,
okay, to see that there is an illusion created by modern materialistic science
by which it presents itself as the authority on the universe.
Okay.
Modern science presents itself as an authority on the universe.
And how do we see through this illusion and how do we see things correctly?
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
As I was explaining earlier, science and technology working together
has created that kind of an immense confidence and trust in people
that science and technology is the most credible source
of knowledge in the world today.
So, that's exactly what has happened.
Science develops to some extent and then the technology takes over, uses some of those
principles and creates things in our life which improves our life and then we start
having tremendous faith in science because from science technology has come, from technology
our life has improved. We see that link.
But if you ask some very very serious scientists in the world, they also admit that science
has its limitations. Science works well in a certain domain, the domain of the material
universe and if you have to investigate things, phenomena outside of this domain, simply the
tools of science break down.
I have given one popular example, I don't know if you have all heard. This is an example
given by Professor Charles Townes.
Professor Charles Townes was a
a
was the inventor of the laser beam, somewhere in the 1960s and he got a Nobel Prize for
physics for his work on lasers. He is a very devout Christian and he has, he made a statement
and he gives an example. Suppose there is a, there is a country, a country
made a statement.
I have a very simple example.
Let's say there is a country with 1000 people. That's the population of the country. And
in that country, they have enough resources to provide good life, housing, health care,
roads, jobs, all of this for only 900 people. But there are 1000 people in the country.

And because of that, extra hundred people, the resources, there is a crunch and there is a certain level of poverty. People are not able to have as much as they would want to have. And there is a certain want.

Now, if you were to ask, look for a scientific solution for this, a very simple, straightforward solution will be, out of that country of one thousand people, you eliminate hundred people, then you will have nine hundred people in the country and you will have enough resources so that everyone can have good life.
Although this is a very scientific solution, it is not a scientific solution.
It is not a scientific answer. Practically, no one will be ready to implement this.
There will be so many more complex questions that come up. On what basis shall we decide these hundred people will be eliminated?
And do we have, there are many moral and ethical questions that come into play.
In other words, Professor Charles Townes is saying that there are certain questions, certain aspects.
Of morality, ethics, and all of those kind of things, which are actually today outside the domain of science.
So you need to, we need to recognize, true scientists, big scientists do recognize that there are in fact certain things which are outside.
It's very difficult to explain.
In fact, I had begun the presentation with.

Stephen Hawking's presentation, Stephen Hawking was not a, he was a kind of an atheist bordering with a mix of agnostic kind of a thing, he was not a believer, he was something, you know, he was, he was the words like mind of God and such things, but he was not too sure about those kind of things.
So you can call him to be something like an agnostic kind of a person.
There is one phenomenon.
There is one phenomenon.
There is one phenomenon.
There is one phenomenon in physics, in science, and I would like to quote Stephen Hawking's comment on that.
This understanding is called fine tuning of the universe.
There are a few constants in science.
For example, the speed of light.
The charge of an electron.
The mass of an electron.
Gravitational constant.
And there are about 15, 20 or 25 constants like that.
These are considered to be very important constants in physics.
And these constants are the way they are.
Like for instance, the charge of an electron or the mass of an electron or the speed of light.
Now, there are a lot of scientists who have worked on this and they have, in fact, even Stephen Hawking had worked on this.
And they have found that if any of these constants were even slightly different by a billion billion, that is 10 power minus 18.
If it was a little different.
The world would not have come about like this.
The world would not have come about like this.
The world would not have come about the way it is.
And we would not have had the galaxies and the solar systems.
We would not have had.
And we would not be sitting like this today and asking questions and thinking about our own existence.
So all of this has happened because those constants were fine tuned.
They were not.
They are not a little bit this way or that way.
Now, how did this fine tuning of the universe happen?
In when when this when this discussion was happening, one of the comments that Stephen Hawking made was that this has serious religious consequences.
So it's a fact that the universe that we live in.
Has has come about even according to science and has certain constants.
And if these constants were even slightly different, we would not have the universe that we have.
You know, it's actually very good that in astronomers have found that there are many stars that come up and they stay for some time and then they burn out.
End of the star.
Death of the star.
Many such phenomena keeps happening in the cosmos.
But our sun is relatively a stable sun and has got a long life.
And they say that even the sun is fading and it'll take, of course, some number of I forget the number.
I think it's about 15 billion more years or so.
Definitely none of us will be there.
So we don't have to worry so much about it.
But it is going to fade one day.
Imagine one day.
Those days, the humanity, how human society will be.
They start observing that the sun is fading.
Just like there will be panic.

Then governments will be enacting national disaster acts and all kinds of, you know, fear and all such things will be happening in the human society because they don't know next day they will wake up and see whether the sun comes or it's gone already dead.
And if the sun goes away, the earth will freeze.
And you can't turn on your job.
You can't turn on your generators.
And, you know, none of those things will work because ultimately they're all dependent on some fossil energy and fossil fuel and all of that.
So how will we live?
We will all die.
People, all people will die due to freezing cold.
So we have a fairly stable sun, which has allowed life to evolve, as science describes, on earth.
And maybe in other places.
In other places in the universe.
And we are able to have a fairly good time leading a stable life.
So all of this is possible because of the fine tuning of the universe.
And how did these constants get fine tuned?
In fact, there is another question.
I'll share this with you once.
Not once.
Actually, I went twice to Yamunotri.
And so when we, I don't know if any of you have gone to Yamunotri, it's a very arduous walk for about six hours.
You go through the hills and either you have to be carried by ponies or you have to be walking and very, very narrow track.
And it's a very arduous trek for about five, six hours.
So when I was trekking like that, there were a few devotees with me.
Both the times I went.
And I was reflecting, why is it Yamunotri is so important?
And finally, we went there and then we had a dip there.
And then we were told that Yamuna starts somewhere up there.
So I said, oh, really?
I thought this is Yamunotri.
This is where Yamuna starts.
No, no, no.
Yamuna starts even further higher and we can't go there.
It's not possible for human beings to walk till there.
And this is the last spot that we recognize.
This is the last spot that we recognize as the source of Yamuna.
So why is Yamunotri important?
Because Yamuna Uttri.
Uttri means that's where it began.
So because we know Yamuna is a very important river, holy river.
Similarly, Ganga Uttri.
Ganga and Yamuna are important rivers in our country and where these rivers begin.
And that's an important place.
That's an important place for all those who regard Yamuna and Ganga are holy.
And so we go and visit, try to visit those places.
Then I was thinking, okay, if Ganga and Yamuna are so important and we want to go and see
their place of origin because it's important for us.
And according to science, the whole universe began with a big bang.
Right?
So the thought came to my mind.
Where did this big bang occur?
And if we were to know that place, that point, and that would be the universe Uttri.
Right?
That's the place where the universe began.
The whole universe should go there too, because that's the holy pilgrimage of the whole for the whole universe.
Because that's where the universe began.
So this thought came to my mind.
And then afterwards I was just reflecting on it.
And then I did some reading.
Actually, it's very interesting.
Where is the point that the big bang occurred?
In fact, there is no point in the universe where the big bang occurred because there was no point in space.
Space itself did not exist.
It is from the big bang space occurred.
Space.
Time.
Energy.
Matter.
Everything came from that big bang.
Then I was doing some more reading.
And then I found that actually what scientists mean by a big bang is when they have tried to create mathematical models for all these things.
And they come up with an equation.
And that equation at t greater than zero has meaningful significance.
And you get to explain how the universe is evolving.
But when you put t equal to zero, you don't have a solution for that equation.
You come to a mathematical situation, what is known as singularity.
You can't solve the equation.
There are no known solutions for that equation.
When t equal to zero, when that equation cannot give results, that is known as the big bang.
Actually, scientists are very good in giving good names.
They give fancy names.
And they are, you know, creative people and intelligent people.
They give good names.
And the common people pick up from that.
And we get the impression that, you know, big bang theory means it's a big bang occurred.
And then we get the feeling that it's like some kind of an explosion, some like a firework, Diwali fireworks or something happening.
In the universe.
And then the universe came about.
Actually, it is simply a mathematical equation at t equal to zero becoming a singularity, having no solutions.
And that's known as the big bang.
So this is what it is.
Now, at t equal to zero, when the big bang occurred or whatever that occurred, no one knows what actually occurred at t equal to zero.
Right?
Because the equation is not giving any solutions.
So at t equal to zero, there was no matter.
There was no space.
There was no time.
There was no energy.
But scientists say laws of physics existed because for t even smallest greater than zero.
The laws of physics start working and leads the evolution of the universe to what it has become.
So what is that situation at t equal to zero when none of these things even matter, energy, space, time didn't exist, but laws that govern them existed?
And where did it exist?
How did it exist?
How did this come about?
These are all big questions, even in science.
So if you see, if you talk to real serious scientists, they all admit that there are many, many, many things that we don't know.
So it is a fact that science, although has works very well in a certain domain and provides very meaningful explanations, which is used by technology to improve things in our life.
But at the really, if you go into the very basics of things.
Science has many things which are not clearly understood.
So if we know some of these things, I think we will understand that, yes, science is doing well, but in a certain domain.
But there are so many other aspects.
A true scientist will admit that we don't know how these things work.
Like, for example, in the realm of consciousness, how consciousness works.
What is conscious?
How come?
How consciousness comes about?
These are all very difficult questions, even in science.
So a veil of illusion that science can provide everything, it's not actually, it is an illusion that we have created.
So we have to get rid of that veil of illusion and see things in the right perspective and understand that.
There are many more complex things in the universe.
Why complex things?
Even a simple, I heard one talk by a Nobel laureate about two years ago.
He had asked, he had brought up the question, very interesting question.
If I were to ask you, what's your mobile number?
And all of you, I'm sure you will tell your mobile number.
It's a long 10 digit number, right?
And you will all remember that and you will tell me.
Of course, you cannot remember somebody else's mobile number, your wife, your child, your husband.
You may find it difficult because you have stored it in your mobile and you look for it.
The question is, how does the human brain store numbers?
Where does it store?
We know how a mobile or a computer stores numbers, right?
This we understand because a decimal number is converted into a digital binary number.
And then you have binary circuits with one and zeros.
And then you can store that one and you can retrieve that one.
We understand how.
Electronic memory works.
How does the human memory work?
Human memory is definitely, human brain is not converting into binary numbers and storing it as one string of one and zero.
How is it working?
Where is it stored?
Because the moment you want to recollect something, you recollect the number.
Actually, we recollect not just number.
Our memory is far more developed.
Say, for instance, you're walking on a street, busy street.
Hundreds of people are walking in front of you.
And you're busily walking.
You're thinking somewhere you have to go.
And in front of you, so many faces are coming.
And you look at the face and you reject.
I don't know this person.
And suddenly imagine you're some close friend.
Somebody comes up there.
Immediately you say, oh.
You see hundreds of faces were coming and your brain was scanning them and rejecting.
I don't know.
I don't know.
I don't know this person.
I don't know this person.
And then suddenly somebody will come.
And immediately it's not just number, recollection of the number.
It's a recollection of a face.
And not just face.
You know the person.
You know his thinking.
You know his emotions.
All of that gets uploaded into our conscious memory.
And we start, hey, he's the person.
Hey, how are you?
You remember his name.
And all of those kind of things.
How does the human memory work?
Where is it working?
These are all very, very complex questions.
Even science is not having answers today.
So we must know that science has its limitations.
And then we will, we will respect science for what it is, for what it is providing.
And at the same time, we won't get carried away thinking that, you know, science is the final word for everything.
Even through genuine scientists, even today.
Except that, we don't know many things.
Okay.
Next question.
All the Vedic knowledge was compressed in the form of Vedanta Sutra.
So what was the need for again expanding it in the form of Srimad Bhagavatam?
Yes.
We discussed about how the history of Vedic knowledge.
The Vedic knowledge,
was compressed,
not the Vedic knowledge.
The last word of Vedic knowledge.
The last,
the ultimate of Vedic wisdom,
Vedanta,
was compressed in the form of Sutras.
So that people can carry that in their memory.
And recollect them and expand on that.
Now, what was the need for again expanding it in the form of Srimad Bhagavatam?
One reason is,
Veda Vyasa and the other sages foresaw
that in Kali Yuga,
this vast Vedic knowledge
and then coming to the Vedanta knowledge
and then being able to comprehend and understand the Vedanta knowledge
is not an easy thing.
First of all, in previous Yugas,
life was long.
And so you can afford to take a slow process of evolution and understanding.
But in Kali Yuga, the life is very short.
It's just 100 years.
Like Prahalad Maharaj analyzes in the Bhagavatam.
And first 15 years goes away in playful activities.
And then some more years of sleeping.
50% of the life is spent sleeping.
And then we have to earn money, maintain our family and all of that.
Finally, how much time is available for us to
actually introspect and understand spiritual matters?
And even at that time,
human life in Kali Yuga is going to be full of disturbances.
You see how for just basic living,
people have to work so hard in Kali Yuga.
And they are very disturbed
because of the kind of lifestyle that we have.
So realizing that,
the sages wanted Vedanta once again to be expanded
and made more easily accessible, understandable
and hence they wanted the Srimad Bhagavatam to come about.
And it was the will of the Lord.
The Lord inspired Vyasadeva to do this.
And hence, Vedanta Sutra, although it was compressed
for the sake of people of Kali Yuga,
for their better comprehension,
for their better understanding,
that Vedanta had to be expanded
in the form of the Srimad Bhagavatam.
And I also mentioned, if you recollect,
that the same Vedanta message was also in a very succinct manner
presented in the Bhagavad Gita.
Now you see,
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's emphasis on the Gita and the Bhagavatam,
how these two are the most important literature
for people of Kali Yuga.
And that is why you see,
Srila Prabhupada so painstakingly
staying up all night,
translating word for word
and the translation and writing purports
for these two books,
the Gita and the Bhagavatam.
And apart from that, he also did Chaitanya Charitamruta.
And a very famous explanation of Srila Prabhupada
of the three books is,
Bhagavad Gita gives the science of God,
Srimad Bhagavatam teaches us love of God,
and Chaitanya Charitamruta teaches us
how to intensify that love of God.
This is such a beautiful explanation by Srila Prabhupada.
The science of God,
love of God,
and intensification of love of God.
And hence these three literatures are sufficient
for us to progress in spiritual life
and go back to Godhead.
And that's why Prabhupada wrote these three books,
very painstakingly he created these three books.
And so that's the reason why the expansion happened.
Next,
because it's already predestined
where you need to reach.
So from that principles,
just do whatever is required to complete job or your work.
Never overwork.
It's already predestined.
Am I correct in the approach Prabhu?
This is referring to the five factors of action,
five factors of success in an action
that Krishna talks about.
Adhisthana,
Kartha,
Karana,
Cheshta,
and Daiva.
Yes,
it is in fact an intelligent question
that what we are destined to get will actually come.
But there is also a certain duty that we all have to perform.
We must do our work dutifully.
And as long as we have done the duty to the best of our ability,
because Cheshta is also one of the factors.
And in the scheme of things,
we have,
it is our role that we have to bring our Cheshta.
That's the way the world is organized.
Unless that Cheshta is there,
that one of the four or five components is there,
you will not have result.
And hence we have to do,
it is ordained on us.
It is our responsibility to bring that Cheshta
in the form of our endeavor,
in the form of our duty.
And there is no need to over endeavor.
Because over endeavor is not going to enhance your result.
So we must do our duty.
We must do as much work as it is needed of us.
And the very famous quote that Srila Prabhupada gave was
stuktasya simhasya
pravishanti na pravishanti mukhem rukhaha
Simha is considered to be the king
in the jungle.
He is the lion.
But even the lion has to go hunting.
Even though the king of the jungle has to go hunting.
If the king of the jungle thinks that
I am the king after all,
I will just lie down here with my mouth open
and some animal will enter in my mouth
and I will eat him.
No.
Nahi stuktasya simhasya pravishanti na pravishanti mukhem rukhaha
A rukha will not come and pravishanti
because you are the king,
you are the king of the jungle,
I will come and you please eat me.
It is not going to happen.
So that is the way the material universe is designed.
We have to do our cheshta,
our effort is also an important factor.
And there is no need to over endeavor
because what we are due to get is already destined.
Another question connected with this only.
Cheshta is
exerted by kartha.
It appears that they are one and the same.
Can you please discern them?
Okay.
Cheshta is exerted by kartha.
Kartha is the doer.
Cheshta is the endeavor.
It is actually the person who has to do the endeavor.
And Krishna has separately pointed out
there is a kartha and there is a cheshta.
In fact,
cheshta is a specific kind of work
that is required for the accomplishment.
So prithak cheshta,
varieties of cheshta are there.
So there may be a doer.
If the doer is not doing the specific kind of an effort
that is needed,
then it will not bring about.
So kartha is alone not enough.
Kartha should do the right thing.
Kartha should do the right effort.
He may do the wrong effort.
A specific effort is required.
If he doesn't bring that,
he is not contributing to the overall thing.
So kartha is separate.
Cheshta is separate.
Cheshta is specific activity.
So I do not think that
they are not one and the same.
They are definitely different.
Next question is,
if you are determined but not consistent,
it is a different attribute.
Is it a different attribute?
Okay.
If you are determined
but you are not consistent in your work,
determination is a feature of the kartha we can say
because he is determined.
There is a person.
He has certain competencies
and he has the will.
He has the determination to do.
So determination we can say
is an attribute of kartha.
And consistently being able to do,
do that work consistently
again and again probably.
Many many things are like that in this world.
You have to do,
like say for instance,
you have to become a good musician.
What do they say?
They say you have to practice,
practice, practice.
You have to do,
consistently do that again and again.
So,
that practice we can say
consistently repeating that
we can say
is part of cheshta,
prithak cheshta.
And so,
they are actually different attributes.
We can look at it that way.
Are the four factors also dependent on daiva?
You see daiva,
Prabhupada also explains in the purport
in the Bhagavad Gita,
daiva is considered to be the super cause.
If the four factors of action are there
and if the fifth factor is not there,
then it will not result in the success.
But suppose daiva is there
and these four factors will somehow come about
and even if there is some lacking in it,
daiva factors is so powerful,
it's because it's called the super cause,
it will result in the success of that work.
I've come across some people,
I remember long, long ago,
there was a life member,
a very rich man.
And then I would go and meet him
and many devotees used to meet him also.
Actually, I used to find out
that he's a very simple man,
not at all very intelligent.
And you ask him some questions or something,
he cannot grasp,
he cannot understand.
But,
very, very rich.
Just look at that situation.
And I used to think,
I know so many people I have seen
are so intelligent,
much, much more intelligent than him,
more hardworking than him,
but making a small fraction of the money that he makes,
struggling to make more money.
So,
you can see here,
his daiva factor is so powerful
and he has to get that result.
So he may not be very intelligent,
he may not be hardworking,
but things will fall in his place.
So that's why daiva is considered to be a super cause
of the four things,
of the five things.
Okay, next question.
Can you please explain
how daivam factor materializes in our activities?
It is very hard to think that a hardworking student,
having all favorable conditions,
mysteriously failed
because of daiva factor.
How does daiva manifest
and how it is related to karma?
Okay.
Somebody may assemble the four factors
very meticulously,
hard work and good situation.
He may try to create a good situation,
hard work and be very determined
and use the right kind of approach
and all of those kind of things.
And
if daiva has not favored that person,
he will not succeed.
Even though the four factors were assembled,
the fifth factor is a very, very important factor.
It can happen.
There are umpteen examples.
How many lakh students write our JEE exam,
entrance exam,
IIT entrance exam?
How many students write that
IIM, Indian Institute of Mechanics tests,
lakhs and lakhs
and only thousands make it?
How many students write IAS exams,
entrance exam or whatever those exams are?
Lakhs of them
and only a few hundred make it.
And they all,
you know, they are aware that they have to work hard,
they have to study,
they have to do this, that,
they use some coaching classes, everything.
But still, daiva was not in their favor.
You see?
So,
sometimes it happens like that.
So, how does daiva manifest,
and how is it related to karma?
Actually, daiva is a manifestation of law of karma,
except for devotees.
For devotees,
they get a certain result,
not strictly according to the law of karma,
because Krishna becomes involved in it.
But otherwise,
by and large,
daiva factor is nothing but the law of karma,
deciding what different living entities should get
in this material world.
So, you can replace daiva with karma,
law of karma.
That is
karmana daivane trena,
that the law of karma is being supervised
and implemented by the Lord
as Paramatma,
seated in the heart of every living entity.
So,
he is playing that role,
except in the case of devotees.
For devotees,
Krishna sees the laws of karma,
and then he also sees the devotees' determination,
wanting to serve,
his surrender to serve the Lord
and the Lord's representative.
Then Krishna will interfere,
even though that karma won't permit him to
experience certain kind of a situation,
Krishna will allow that to happen,
because
he is serving the Lord's mission.
This can happen only for bhaktas,
for devotees,
who are trying to please Krishna,
who are serving Krishna's mission.
For them,
Krishna manifests this exemption clause.
Otherwise,
everybody is strictly under the laws of karma,
from an ant to a devata,
to a big demigod.
Everyone is governed by laws of karma.
So,
this is the next question.
How soul,
which is finer than the all eight elements,
be enclosed in material body,
just like air,
which is finer,
if you try enclosing in jar,
it will escape.
How is then,
this soul,
which is finer,
is in the body?
Okay.
Yes,
the soul is very fine,
finer than all the five elements,
gross,
then mind,
finer than intelligence,
finer than soul,
finer than false ego,
and then is the soul.
So,
the soul,
by the arrangement of the Lord,
is embodied,
is residing in this body.
It is by the laws of nature.
That is why,
according to that daiva,
according to the Lord's arrangement,
the soul can stay in this body
exactly as
for the period that the Lord has determined.
You cannot extend the life of a person
beyond
what he is destined to do.
There is one saying that
number of
we inhale and exhale, right?
So,
even unconsciously,
we keep breathing in and breathing out.
It is said that
number of times
we breathe in
and number of times
we breathe out,
the count
is already determined.
And,
so,
it is by a divine intervention
that the soul has been encapsulated
into this gross and subtle body.
